blueprints.name: Approval Chain
blueprints.probeQuestion: Does this process involve a human approver who reviews a submission and can approve, reject, or request changes?
blueprints.domainDescription: Human-initiated approval workflows with human decision-makers

roles.description: The actor who initiates or submits a request for approval
The actor who reviews, approves, or authorizes the request

expectedScenarios.description: Approver rejects or declines the request
expectedScenarios.questionTemplate: What happens if {approver.actor} rejects or declines at step {approver.stepIndex} ("{approver.description}")? Can they request changes instead of outright rejection?

expectedScenarios.description: Approver approves with conditions or requires re-approval
expectedScenarios.questionTemplate: Can {approver.actor} approve with conditions at step {approver.stepIndex}? What changes require re-approval?

expectedScenarios.description: Approver is unavailable or not authorized, and approval is delegated
expectedScenarios.questionTemplate: What if {approver.actor} is unavailable or not the expected authority at step {approver.stepIndex}? Can someone else approve or delegate?

blueprints.name: Request Fulfillment Lifecycle
blueprints.probeQuestion: Does this process involve an initiator submitting a request that is later fulfilled, delivered, or resolved by another actor or system?
blueprints.domainDescription: Human-initiated requests fulfilled by humans or systems

roles.description: The actor who initiates a request, order, or claim
The actor or party who fulfills, delivers, or resolves the request

expectedScenarios.description: Initiator cancels or withdraws the request after submission
expectedScenarios.questionTemplate: Can {initiator.actor} cancel or withdraw the request after step {initiator.stepIndex} ("{initiator.description}")? What happens to downstream processing?

expectedScenarios.description: Initiator modifies the request during processing
expectedScenarios.questionTemplate: Can {initiator.actor} change the request after submission at step {initiator.stepIndex}? Do modifications require re-approval or re-evaluation?

expectedScenarios.description: Request is only partially fulfilled
expectedScenarios.questionTemplate: What if {fulfiller.actor} only partially fulfills the request at step {fulfiller.stepIndex} ("{fulfiller.description}")? How is the remainder handled?

expectedScenarios.description: Fulfillment does not match the request
expectedScenarios.questionTemplate: What if what {fulfiller.actor} delivers at step {fulfiller.stepIndex} does not match what was requested? Who verifies and how is it resolved?

expectedScenarios.description: Fulfillment is delayed beyond expected timeframe
expectedScenarios.questionTemplate: What happens if {fulfiller.actor} does not fulfill by the expected time at step {fulfiller.stepIndex}? Is there a deadline or alert mechanism?

blueprints.name: Multi-Party Selection
blueprints.probeQuestion: Does this process involve an actor choosing among multiple options, vendors, providers, or candidates — where no match or multiple matches may be found?
blueprints.domainDescription: Human actor selects among options, possibly involving system recommendations

roles.description: The actor who selects among multiple options or parties

expectedScenarios.description: Request is split across multiple entities
expectedScenarios.questionTemplate: What if the request at step {selector.stepIndex} requires multiple entities? Can {selector.actor} split the request across multiple parties?

expectedScenarios.description: Multiple requests are merged for efficiency
expectedScenarios.questionTemplate: Can {selector.actor} merge multiple similar requests at step {selector.stepIndex}? How are merged items tracked?

expectedScenarios.description: No suitable option is found
expectedScenarios.questionTemplate: What if {selector.actor} cannot find a suitable match at step {selector.stepIndex}? Is there a fallback or alternative source?

blueprints.name: Information Completeness
blueprints.probeQuestion: Does this process require an actor to provide or fill in detailed information, where some fields may be unknown, missing, or filled in later by another actor?
blueprints.domainDescription: Human provides information to system, handling incomplete data scenarios

roles.description: The actor who provides required information

expectedScenarios.description: Required information is unknown or unavailable
expectedScenarios.questionTemplate: What if {provider.actor} does not know all required information at step {provider.stepIndex} ("{provider.description}")? Can fields be left blank and filled later?

expectedScenarios.description: Missing information is filled in later by another actor
expectedScenarios.questionTemplate: If information is missing at step {provider.stepIndex}, who fills it in later and does this require re-approval or re-evaluation?

blueprints.name: API Integration Pattern
blueprints.probeQuestion: Does this process involve one system calling another system's API or service, where timeouts, unavailability, or bad responses may occur?
blueprints.domainDescription: System-to-system communication via APIs with error handling

roles.description: The system that initiates an API call or request
The system that processes the request and returns a response

expectedScenarios.description: API call times out without response
expectedScenarios.questionTemplate: What happens if {responder.actor} does not respond within the timeout period at step {responder.stepIndex}? Does {caller.actor} retry or fail?

expectedScenarios.description: Target service is unavailable or down
expectedScenarios.questionTemplate: What if {responder.actor} is unavailable at step {responder.stepIndex}? Is there a fallback service or circuit breaker?

expectedScenarios.description: API returns unexpected or malformed data
expectedScenarios.questionTemplate: What if {responder.actor} returns malformed or unexpected data at step {responder.stepIndex}? How does {caller.actor} handle schema validation failures?

expectedScenarios.description: API rate limit exceeded
expectedScenarios.questionTemplate: What if {caller.actor} exceeds rate limits at step {caller.stepIndex}? Is there retry logic with exponential backoff?

blueprints.name: Data Synchronization Pattern
blueprints.probeQuestion: Does this process involve synchronizing data between two or more systems, where conflicts, failures, or stale data may occur?
blueprints.domainDescription: Systems synchronizing data across databases or services

roles.description: The system that provides or publishes data
The system that receives and processes synchronized data

expectedScenarios.description: Conflicting updates from multiple sources
expectedScenarios.questionTemplate: What if {target_system.actor} receives conflicting updates at step {target_system.stepIndex}? How is conflict resolution handled (last-write-wins, versioning)?

expectedScenarios.description: Synchronization fails due to network or system error
expectedScenarios.questionTemplate: What if synchronization fails at step {target_system.stepIndex}? Does {source_system.actor} retry or queue updates?

expectedScenarios.description: Only partial data is synchronized successfully
expectedScenarios.questionTemplate: What if only partial data is synchronized at step {target_system.stepIndex}? How is data consistency maintained?

expectedScenarios.description: Target system has stale or outdated data
expectedScenarios.questionTemplate: What if {target_system.actor} has stale data at step {target_system.stepIndex}? Is there a mechanism to detect and refresh outdated records?

blueprints.name: Event-Driven Processing Pattern
blueprints.probeQuestion: Does this process involve a system publishing events or messages that another system consumes asynchronously, where events may be lost, duplicated, or arrive out of order?
blueprints.domainDescription: Asynchronous event publishing and consumption between systems

roles.description: The system that publishes or emits events
The system that subscribes to and processes events

expectedScenarios.description: Event is lost before being consumed
expectedScenarios.questionTemplate: What if an event published by {publisher.actor} at step {publisher.stepIndex} is lost? Is there guaranteed delivery or dead-letter queue?

expectedScenarios.description: Same event is processed multiple times
expectedScenarios.questionTemplate: What if {subscriber.actor} receives duplicate events at step {subscriber.stepIndex}? Is there idempotency handling?

expectedScenarios.description: Events are consumed out of order
expectedScenarios.questionTemplate: What if {subscriber.actor} receives events out of order at step {subscriber.stepIndex}? Is ordering guaranteed or does the system handle reordering?

expectedScenarios.description: Subscriber fails to process event
expectedScenarios.questionTemplate: What if {subscriber.actor} fails to process an event at step {subscriber.stepIndex}? Is there retry logic or compensation?

blueprints.name: Batch Processing Pattern
blueprints.probeQuestion: Does this process involve a system executing batch or bulk operations over datasets, where partial failures, timeouts, or duplicate runs may occur?
blueprints.domainDescription: Automated batch jobs processing large datasets across systems

roles.description: The system that executes batch processing jobs

expectedScenarios.description: Some records in batch fail while others succeed
expectedScenarios.questionTemplate: What if only some records succeed at step {batch_processor.stepIndex}? Does {batch_processor.actor} rollback successful records or continue?

expectedScenarios.description: Batch job exceeds time limit
expectedScenarios.questionTemplate: What if {batch_processor.actor} cannot complete within time window at step {batch_processor.stepIndex}? Can it resume or must it restart?

expectedScenarios.description: Batch job runs twice due to scheduling error
expectedScenarios.questionTemplate: What if {batch_processor.actor} runs twice at step {batch_processor.stepIndex}? Is there duplicate detection or idempotency?

blueprints.name: Resource Locking & Concurrency Control
blueprints.probeQuestion: Does this process involve shared resources that multiple clients or systems may access concurrently, requiring locking, contention handling, or deadlock prevention?
blueprints.domainDescription: System-to-system resource access control, lock management, and concurrency handling

roles.description: The system component that manages resource access, grants/releases locks, and handles contention
The system or service that requests access to shared resources

expectedScenarios.description: Multiple clients compete for the same resource
expectedScenarios.questionTemplate: What happens when multiple clients try to access the same resource at step {resource_manager.stepIndex}? How does {resource_manager.actor} handle contention?

expectedScenarios.description: Client wants to upgrade from shared to exclusive access
expectedScenarios.questionTemplate: What if a client at step {resource_manager.stepIndex} wants to upgrade from shared to exclusive access? Can {resource_manager.actor} safely convert the lock?

expectedScenarios.description: Circular wait for resources could cause deadlock
expectedScenarios.questionTemplate: How does {resource_manager.actor} prevent deadlock at step {resource_manager.stepIndex} when multiple resources are involved? Is there deadlock detection?

expectedScenarios.description: Client holds lock for too long without releasing
expectedScenarios.questionTemplate: What if a client holds the lock indefinitely after step {resource_manager.stepIndex}? Does {resource_manager.actor} have a timeout mechanism?

expectedScenarios.description: All resources are locked/unavailable
expectedScenarios.questionTemplate: What happens at step {resource_manager.stepIndex} when all resources are locked? How does {resource_manager.actor} handle exhaustion?

expectedScenarios.description: Client fails or crashes while holding lock
expectedScenarios.questionTemplate: What if the client crashes or fails while holding a lock after step {resource_manager.stepIndex}? How does {resource_manager.actor} recover the lock?

expectedScenarios.description: High-priority client needs access while resource is locked
expectedScenarios.questionTemplate: What if a high-priority client requests access at step {resource_manager.stepIndex} while the resource is locked? Can {resource_manager.actor} preempt the current lock?

blueprints.name: Session Persistence & Recovery
blueprints.probeQuestion: Can users save their work mid-process and resume later, or recover if the session is interrupted, timed out, or unexpectedly terminated?
blueprints.domainDescription: User can save, pause, resume, and restart interrupted work

roles.description: The actor who performs work that may be interrupted
The system that manages session state and recovery

expectedScenarios.description: User saves work in progress to continue later
expectedScenarios.questionTemplate: Can {user.actor} save their work at step {user.stepIndex} ("{user.description}") to continue later? What happens to incomplete data?

expectedScenarios.description: User pauses or temporarily stops work
expectedScenarios.questionTemplate: What happens if {user.actor} needs to pause at step {user.stepIndex}? Is their progress preserved?

expectedScenarios.description: User resumes previously saved or paused work
expectedScenarios.questionTemplate: Can {user.actor} resume their work from where they left off? How does the system restore the previous state?

expectedScenarios.description: User needs to restart work after unexpected interruption
expectedScenarios.questionTemplate: What if {user.actor}'s work is unexpectedly interrupted at step {user.stepIndex} (network failure, browser crash, timeout)? Can they restart or must they start over?

expectedScenarios.description: User's session expires due to inactivity
expectedScenarios.questionTemplate: What happens if {user.actor}'s session times out during step {user.stepIndex}? Is their work saved automatically?

expectedScenarios.description: System automatically saves user's progress
expectedScenarios.questionTemplate: Does the system automatically save {user.actor}'s progress during step {user.stepIndex}? How frequently?
